description: "Use when working on Router Certification Tranche, HLF orchestration, capsule validation, ALIGN checks, gas accounting, or SSOT-backed governed agent execution."
name: "Router Certification Tranche"
---

# Router Certification Tranche

- Load [HLF SSOT](../../HLF%20SSOT%20%E2%80%94%20Complete%20LLM%20Briefing%20Document%20Hieroglyphic%20Logic%20Framework%20%C2%B7%20Single%20Source%20of%20Truth%20for%20NLP%20Environments%20Verified%20against_%20hlf_mcp_hlf_grammar.md) before producing HLF, capsule plans, or certification judgments.
- Treat the SSOT as the local authoritative backing for virtual `GET hlf://<resource_path>` lookups.
- Use the prototype interaction contract: user speaks NLP, agent works in HLF terms, agent reports back in NLP unless raw HLF is explicitly required.
- Treat GrumpRolled as the default proving ground for whether HLF is useful in a real product workflow.
- Within GrumpRolled, default to the bounded experiment claim: HLF is being tested here as a communication, translation, governed-programming, and audit surface, not automatically as a fully attached upstream Python runtime.
- Load the Router Certification reference pack before non-trivial use so claim lanes, current truth, build-assist boundaries, and GrumpRolled use-case fit are explicit.
- Preserve HLF current-truth wording discipline: distinguish packaged current-proof surfaces from doctrine/target-state language and bridge/recovery aspirations.
- Do not claim live HLF runtime execution, remote recursive-build maturity, or self-hosting proof unless those capabilities are actually available and verified in the current environment.
- If no live HLF runtime or MCP server is attached, present outputs as SSOT-backed simulation, not as executed runtime evidence.
- Do not describe local GrumpRolled proof slices as evidence of the full upstream MCP/runtime stack unless the local environment actually exercises that stack end to end.
- Preserve the HLF contract: determinism, zero-trust execution, cryptographic auditing, token-efficiency, capsule pre-flight, gas accounting, ALIGN enforcement, and ethical-governor refusal behavior.
- Prefer real packaged HLF surface names when relevant: `hlf_do`, `hlf_compile`, `hlf_validate`, `hlf_run`, `hlf_translate_to_english`, `hlf_capsule_validate`, `hlf_memory_query`, `hlf_instinct_step`, and `hlf_test_suite_summary`.
- Prefer experiments tied to actual GrumpRolled surfaces such as forums, trust, moderation, knowledge, A2A routing, or federation so usefulness can be proven or falsified.
- Do not force Router Certification onto basic CRUD, pagination, styling, or routine plumbing that lacks trust, governance, or proof boundaries.
- Do not improvise capabilities outside the SSOT. If a required opcode, host function, capsule privilege, or governance allowance is missing, refuse or narrow the task.
- When generating HLF or certification outputs, include the explicit audit surface: tier, gas estimate, constraints, refusal reason if blocked, and human-readable summary.
